intent: Fix critical+high WCAG/UX blockers found by Bird (UX-REVIEW.md) on the Gmail Automation Dashboard: icon-button aria-labels, mobile reflow, modal dialog semantics, lang/title/h1, focus ring, contrast, tap targets, input labels, keyboard assignment path. Bird re-verifies over CDP. Driven by Kermit -> Oscar (Bert/Telly/Bird).
scope: src/ui/dashboard-html/** (Wave 1, collision-free), src/ui/dashboardController.js + dashboard-api.js (Wave 2, serialized after coverage leg, keep 100%), UX-REVIEW.md, tests-local/ (only after coverage leg done)

fixes: lang=en + <title> + charset/viewport meta in DashboardMain head
<nav aria-label> label rail + <section aria-label> categories landmarks
all 6 modals -> role=dialog aria-modal aria-labelledby; close buttons aria-label
44x44 min tap targets for icon buttons

wave1b.note: dynamic icon-button aria-labels in client-side HTML partials (dashboard-labels.html, dashboard-categories.html) - collision-free, resolves Bird's 77-unnamed-buttons critical

wave1c.note: modal focus management (setupModalA11y in dashboard-core.html) - focus move-in, Tab trap, Escape + backdrop close, focus restore. Collision-free client JS.

applied: dashboardController.js createCategoryPill: added aria-label="Remove ${displayName} from ${labelName}" to the remove-X <button> in its innerHTML (resolves Bird's unnamed dynamic remove-X button finding).
Extended existing createCategoryPill test in tests-local/dashboardController.test.js to assert the aria-label is present (no new branch -> coverage stays 100%).
no_change: dashboard-api.js doGet: confirmed it sets setTitle('Email Tools Dashboard'). The inner-iframe lang/title/h1 + viewport meta were fixed in Wave 1 DashboardMain.html; the served template already carries <meta name=viewport>. Adding .addMetaTag('viewport',...) in doGet would be redundant -> left unchanged per plan.
wave2.keyboard_dnd_alternative: DEFERRED to backlog (see backlog[]). Bird High: existing modals already give keyboard paths for adding domains/emails to a category and choosing a label at category creation; only reassigning an EXISTING category to a different label is drag-only. A keyboard alternative needs a new UI affordance (per-pill move menu/buttons) + new DOM + new client event wiring + tests, which meaningfully expands the leg's scope. Deferred rather than blow the leg open.

wave3.note: Chrome CDP was DOWN at re-verify (curl http://localhost:9222/json/version unreachable). Did NOT block. Statically re-confirmed every Wave 1/1b/1c/2 fix against the committed HTML/CSS/JS partials + existing tests-ux artifacts; wrote 'Re-verification (post-fix)' before/after section in UX-REVIEW.md. Live axe-in-iframe re-run PENDING a Chrome relaunch (bash tests-ux/launch-chrome-cdp.sh; node tests-ux/bird-drive.js).
wave3.static_evidence: DashboardMain.html lang/title/viewport/charset + nav/section landmarks; DashboardHeader.html h1 + 2 input aria-labels; DashboardModals.html 6x role=dialog/aria-modal + 19 aria-labels; dashboard-core.html setupModalA11y (focus move-in, Tab trap, Escape, backdrop, restore); DashboardStyles.html :focus-visible gold ring, contrast tokens >=4.5:1, 44px tap targets, @media max-width:640px single-column reflow; dashboard-categories.html + dashboard-labels.html dynamic aria-labels (escapeAttr); dashboardController.js createCategoryPill aria-label (Wave 2).

backlog.title: Keyboard-accessible alternative to drag-and-drop for reassigning an EXISTING category to a different label
backlog.why_deferred: Adding a keyboard path requires a new per-pill move affordance (menu/buttons) + DOM + client event wiring + matching tests to keep the 100% gate; touches server moveCategoryBetweenLabels indirectly. Larger than the Wave 2 a11y-label scope; deferred to avoid expanding the leg.

concurrency_guard: full-test-coverage leg running in parallel owns session.json + src/**/*.js + tests-local/**; this leg uses session-ux-fix.json; Wave 2 (.js) waits for coverage completion (poll HANDOFF.json last_leg==full-test-coverage)
